body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge` **Service directory:** `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/` **Triggered by:** PR #57614 by @haiyuazhang ## What Changed PR #57614 migrated `Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge` from AutoRest/Swagger to TypeSpec (MPG migration). The migration introduced: - **3 new resource types**: `DataBoxEdgeDeviceCapacityInfoResource`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceNetworkSettingsResource`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceUpdateSummaryResource` - **New custom models**: `DataBoxEdgeDeviceCapacityInfo`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceNetworkSettings`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceUpdateSummary`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleAddonData`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleData`, `DataBoxEdgeTriggerData`, `EdgeFileEventTrigger`, `EdgeProfileSubscription`, `PeriodicTimerEventTrigger` - **Significant API surface changes** to `DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleResource`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleAddonResource`, `DataBoxEdgeOrderResource`, and other existing resource types - **Modified enum type**: `DataBoxEdgeEncryptionAlgorithm` and `DataBoxEdgeSkuName` were reworked (43 and 116 deletions respectively) ## Gaps Found - **CHANGELOG `1.2.0-beta.1` section is empty** — the section exists but has no entries under "Features Added", "Breaking Changes", "Bugs Fixed", or "Other Changes" despite substantial API additions and removals - **README "Key concepts" section is generic** — links only to external Azure SDK docs with no DataBoxEdge-specific content describing available resource types (`DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource`, `BandwidthScheduleResource`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleResource`, etc.) - **README "Examples" section has no code samples** — only links to a generic `https://aka.ms/azuresdk-net-mgmt-samples` URL; no DataBoxEdge-specific snippets for common operations - **New resource types are undocumented** — `DataBoxEdgeDeviceCapacityInfoResource`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceNetworkSettingsResource`, and `DataBoxEdgeDeviceUpdateSummaryResource` are not mentioned in README or CHANGELOG - **No `Samples/` directory** in the tests folder — no test-backed snippet files exist at all
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/CHANGELOG.md` — **edit** Update the `1.2.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` section to document the changes introduced by the TypeSpec migration: ```markdown ## 1.2.0-beta.1 (Unreleased) ### Features Added - Added new resource type `DataBoxEdgeDeviceCapacityInfoResource` exposing device capacity information. - Added new resource type `DataBoxEdgeDeviceNetworkSettingsResource` exposing device network settings. - Added new resource type `DataBoxEdgeDeviceUpdateSummaryResource` exposing device update summary. - Added new model types: `DataBoxEdgeDeviceCapacityInfo`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceNetworkSettings`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceUpdateSummary`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleAddonData`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleData`, `DataBoxEdgeTriggerData`, `EdgeFileEventTrigger`, `EdgeProfileSubscription`, `PeriodicTimerEventTrigger`. - Migrated the library code generation from AutoRest/Swagger to TypeSpec. ### Breaking Changes - `DataBoxEdgeEncryptionAlgorithm` enum values have been updated to align with the TypeSpec-generated API. - `DataBoxEdgeSkuName` enum values have been updated to align with the TypeSpec-generated API. ``` #### File 2: `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/README.md` — **edit** Replace the generic "Key concepts" and "Examples" sections with DataBoxEdge-specific content. Replace the existing "Key concepts" section body: ```markdown ## Key concepts Key concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/dotnet_introduction.html). ``` with: ```markdown ## Key concepts The `Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge` library provides access to the following main resource types: - **`DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource`**: Represents a Data Box Edge physical device. Supports CRUD operations as well as actions such as scanning for updates, triggering updates, and retrieving security settings. - **`BandwidthScheduleResource`**: Manages bandwidth schedules on a device to control network usage during specified time windows. - **`DataBoxEdgeRoleResource`**: Manages compute roles (e.g., IoT, Kubernetes) running on the device. - **`DataBoxEdgeRoleAddonResource`**: Manages add-ons associated with a role (e.g., Arc for Kubernetes). - **`DataBoxEdgeShareResource`**: Manages data shares (SMB or NFS) on the device. - **`DataBoxEdgeStorageAccountResource`**: Manages storage accounts configured on the device. - **`DataBoxEdgeDeviceCapacityInfoResource`**: Provides information about device resource capacity utilization. - **`DataBoxEdgeDeviceNetworkSettingsResource`**: Provides read access to the device's network configuration. - **`DataBoxEdgeDeviceUpdateSummaryResource`**: Provides a summary of available updates for the device. For general Azure Resource Manager SDK concepts, see the [Azure Resource Manager documentation](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/dotnet_introduction.html). ``` Replace the existing "Examples" section body: ```markdown ## Examples Code samples for using the management library for .NET can be found in the following locations - [.NET Management Library Code Samples](https://aka.ms/azuresdk-net-mgmt-samples) ``` with: ```markdown ## Examples The following sections show code snippets for common scenarios. ### List all Data Box Edge devices in a subscription ```C# Snippet:DataBoxEdge_ListDevices ``` ### Get a specific Data Box Edge device ```C# Snippet:DataBoxEdge_GetDevice ``` ### List bandwidth schedules for a device ```C# Snippet:DataBoxEdge_ListBandwidthSchedules ``` For more samples, see the [Azure SDK for .NET management samples](https://aka.ms/azuresdk-net-mgmt-samples). ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Create a new test file with sample code that the snippet tooling will inject into the README. #### New file: `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/tests/Samples/Sample1_DataBoxEdgeDevice.cs` ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System; using System.Threading.Tasks; using Azure.Identity; using Azure.ResourceManager; using Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge; using Azure.ResourceManager.Resources; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge.Tests.Samples { public class Sample1_DataBoxEdgeDevice { [Test] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample builds")] public async Task ListDevices() { #region Snippet:DataBoxEdge_ListDevices ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); await foreach (DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource device in subscription.GetDataBoxEdgeDevicesAsync()) { Console.WriteLine($"Device: {device.Data.Name}, Location: {device.Data.Location}"); } #endregion } [Test] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample builds")] public async Task GetDevice() { #region Snippet:DataBoxEdge_GetDevice ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync("myResourceGroup"); DataBoxEdgeDeviceCollection devices = resourceGroup.GetDataBoxEdgeDevices(); DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource device = await devices.GetAsync("myDataBoxEdgeDevice"); Console.WriteLine($"Device: {device.Data.Name}"); Console.WriteLine($" Model: {device.Data.ModelDescription}"); Console.WriteLine($" Status: {device.Data.DataBoxEdgeDeviceStatus}"); #endregion } [Test] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample builds")] public async Task ListBandwidthSchedules() { #region Snippet:DataBoxEdge_ListBandwidthSchedules ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync("myResourceGroup"); DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource device = await resourceGroup.GetDataBoxEdgeDevices().GetAsync("myDataBoxEdgeDevice"); await foreach (BandwidthScheduleResource schedule in device.GetBandwidthSchedules()) { Console.WriteLine($"Schedule: {schedule.Data.Name}"); } #endregion } } } ``` **README snippet placeholder locations:** - Section: `## Examples` → `### List all Data Box Edge devices in a subscription` - Snippet name: `DataBoxEdge_ListDevices` - Section: `## Examples` → `### Get a specific Data Box Edge device` - Snippet name: `DataBoxEdge_GetDevice` - Section: `## Examples` → `### List bandwidth schedules for a device` - Snippet name: `DataBoxEdge_ListBandwidthSchedules` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/README.md` currently has all required sections. After the edits above, verify: 1. ✅ **Getting started** — present (Install, Prerequisites, Authenticate) 2. ⚠️ **Key concepts** — needs updating (replace generic link with service-specific content, as described in Step 1) 3. ⚠️ **Examples** — needs updating (add snippet placeholders and create sample test file, as described in Steps 1–2) 4. ✅ **Troubleshooting** — present 5. ✅ **Next steps** — present 6. ✅ **Contributing** — present Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 databoxedge` — injects snippet code from test file into README placeholders; check git diff to confirm README was updated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 databoxedge` — not required unless public types were added beyond what the migration already exported 5. `dotnet format sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [DataBoxEdge] Docs: Update CHANGELOG and README after MPG migration to TypeSpec
